primary_diagnosis.name: Dental (odontogenic) abscess with spreading facial cellulitis and trismus

pathophysiology_summary: A localized dental (periapical) abscess has progressed beyond the tooth root into the surrounding soft tissues, causing facial cellulitis and swelling. The development of trismus (limited jaw opening) indicates involvement of the muscles of mastication and spread toward the deep fascial spaces of the neck. If untreated, this infection can progress to Ludwig's angina — a rapidly spreading bilateral submandibular space infection that can compress and obstruct the airway, a life-threatening emergency. The combination of rapid swelling progression, trismus, and fever in this case mandates urgent in-person assessment (ED or maxillofacial/dental emergency service) for examination, imaging if indicated, and likely IV antibiotics, rather than relying on oral antibiotics prescribed remotely.

differential.evidence_basis: Throbbing tooth pain with facial swelling and trismus.

differential.diagnosis: Simple localized dental abscess without spread
differential.evidence_basis: Would be less urgent if confined to the tooth without facial swelling or trismus, but these features indicate spread.

differential_progression.scoring_note: Facial swelling with trismus should immediately distinguish this from a simple, uncomplicated dental abscess.

differential.diagnosis: Dental abscess with spreading facial cellulitis and trismus, at risk of deep space spread
differential.evidence_basis: Rapid progression (DI-001) and trismus (DI-002) together confirm spreading infection requiring urgent in-person assessment.

differential_progression.scoring_note: By this stage, urgent same-day in-person assessment should be clearly recommended, not just remote antibiotic prescribing.

differential.diagnosis: Dental abscess with spreading facial cellulitis and trismus
differential.evidence_basis: Working diagnosis requiring urgent in-person assessment, likely IV antibiotics, and dental/maxillofacial referral for source control.

differential_progression.scoring_note: A plan offering only phone-prescribed oral antibiotics without urgent in-person assessment is inadequate given the trismus and rapid progression.

red_flags.description: Rapidly progressive facial swelling over 12 hours
red_flags.clinical_significance: Indicates active spread of infection beyond the local dental site.
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: Progression to deep neck space infection and airway compromise.

red_flags.clinical_significance: Suggests involvement of the muscles of mastication and spread toward deep fascial spaces.
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: Progression to Ludwig's angina with airway obstruction.

red_flags.description: Fluctuant gingival swelling with purulent exudate and a fever of 38.3°C on examination (clinician-only findings)
red_flags.clinical_significance: Confirms active bacterial infection requiring source control.
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: Not directly available to the AI Doctor via chat.

diagnostic_reasoning_pitfalls.pitfall_name: Treating this as a simple toothache manageable with remote antibiotic prescribing alone
diagnostic_reasoning_pitfalls.description: AI Doctor might default to prescribing oral antibiotics over the phone for 'a bad tooth infection' without recognising that facial swelling and trismus indicate a more dangerous spreading infection requiring in-person assessment and possible IV treatment.

cannot_determine_via_chat: Direct examination of facial swelling extent
Assessment of tongue floor elevation/uvula deviation
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.correct_escalation_action: Urgent same-day in-person assessment (ED or urgent dental/maxillofacial service) for examination and likely IV antibiotics; immediate 000 if airway symptoms develop
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.premature_diagnosis_risk: Assuming this is a simple toothache and prescribing oral antibiotics remotely risks missing a rapidly progressing deep space infection.
